_note: Updated by Creative after each task. Plain language — no jargon.

priorities.title: Write the Ashen Hollow art bible foundation — palette, silhouette rules, visual identity
priorities.note: Canonical art bible is v0.2 at artifacts/ashen-hollow-art-bible-v0.2.md with embedded swatches, SVG figures, and concept plates under artifacts/art-bible/. v0.1 is a superseded stub.
priorities.proposed_solution: Use v0.2 as the quality gate; regenerate swatches with scripts/generate_art_bible_swatches.py when palette tokens change.

priorities.title: Design the player character's visual identity — silhouette, palette assignment, animation readiness
priorities.note: The player character appears in every session. Their visual design sets the standard for all other character work. Blocked on game director finalizing the player fantasy and game systems locking the movement spec.
priorities.proposed_solution: After the player fantasy and movement spec lock, define the hero’s silhouette, palette assignment, and animation-ready proportions.

priorities.title: Art bible v0.2 — visual-integrated foundation
priorities.note: Shipped v0.2: per-token PNG swatches, biome mood grid, materials board, silhouette plates, lighting/value/separation SVGs. Concepts in artifacts/art-bible/concepts/; AI-generated—human gate before production.
priorities.proposed_solution: Lock v0.3 items (room crosswalk, pixel export spec) after Game Director / Narrative sign-off.

risks_p1.title: Art bible adoption drift risk
risks_p1.note: The v0.1 bible now exists, but consistency risk remains unless all specialists and contributors actively apply the quality gate during asset reviews.

actions.name: Art Bible Update
actions.description: Updated art bible section — palette assignment, silhouette rules, consistency constraints, atmospheric notes for a new biome or character.
actions.trigger: A new biome, character, or visual system is approved for production

actions.name: Asset Review
actions.description: Three-question review — palette correct? Silhouette reads at game scale? Fits the established visual context? Pass / conditional / reject.

actions.name: AI Art Pipeline Spec
actions.description: LoRA training spec, prompt architecture, negative prompt list, and validation checklist for AI-assisted art production.
actions.trigger: AI-assisted asset generation is being set up for a new asset type or character

opportunities.title: Define the visual language for the first biome — environment tiles, atmospheric palette, hazard readability
opportunities.note: Blocked on game director confirming the first biome. Once confirmed, this is the first substantial art production task and gates all level design work for that biome.
opportunities.proposed_solution: As soon as the first biome is confirmed, specify tiles, atmosphere, and hazard readability for that biome’s environments.

opportunities.title: Establish the AI art generation pipeline spec for background assets
opportunities.note: AI-assisted background generation can significantly accelerate production. The pipeline spec (LoRA training, prompt architecture, validation criteria) should be ready before production volume increases.
opportunities.proposed_solution: Document the AI background pipeline—LoRA, prompts, negatives, and validation checks—before scaling AI-generated environments.
